concerns.text: FR-003 states the system MUST compute 'target salience (as provided in stimulus metadata)', but the Acceptance Scenario 2 in User Story 1 and the Assumptions section explicitly state that if raw salience maps are missing, the system MUST compute them on-the-fly. This creates a contradiction: FR-003 mandates reliance on provided metadata, while the system behavior described elsewhere mandates derivation when metadata is absent. FR-003 should be updated to reflect the derivation logic or the requirement should be split.

FR-007 and SC-003 mandate that the system MUST achieve ≥75% accuracy and ≥0.75 AUC. This is a performance outcome requirement, not a functional capability requirement. A system can be functionally complete (implementing the classifier) yet fail to meet these specific accuracy thresholds due to data quality or model limitations. Framing a statistical result as a 'System MUST' functional requirement creates a logical contradiction where the system is 'broken' if the science yields a negative result. These should be Success Criteria (SC) only, not Functional Requirements (FR).

The warning message in User Story 1 Acceptance Scenario 2 is excessively verbose and includes internal logic ('See FR-003 for the specific dispersion-based fixation detector...'). User-facing or log messages should be concise. Additionally, the text repeats the exact same long string in the 'Assumptions' section, suggesting a copy-paste error rather than distinct content.

User Story 3 describes a 'sliding-window logistic-regression classifier that updates every 200 ms'. However, the Independent Test and Acceptance Scenarios describe running the classifier on a 'held-out subset' or 'test set' (offline batch processing). There is a scope shift between the 'real-time' claim in the story title/description and the 'offline evaluation' described in the test criteria. The spec needs to clarify if the 'real-time' aspect is a simulation of real-time on historical data or a requirement for a live stream interface.
